WWW-Authenticate.description: Optional RFC 6750 Bearer challenge on 401 responses

NotFoundProblem.description: Endpoint or resource was not found

AdminConfigPatchRequest.description:
Fixed, exhaustive PATCH whitelist. Absence retains; `null` resets
a field to its backend-owned default; a value sets it.
`secure_path` is the sole set-only setting.

AdminConfigView.description:
GET config may contain all groups or exactly one requested group. The
optimistic-concurrency revision is always present.

AdminFilterNumber.description:
JSON number preserving the integer/unsigned/decimal distinction needed by
the filter DSL's second-pass column validation.

AdminKnowledgeCategoryName.description:
One administrator-visible knowledge category name. The user endpoint has
an object projection; the administrator endpoint deliberately returns bare
strings and therefore has a distinct contract.

AdminOrderPatchRequest.description:
Exactly one order state field is accepted. The closed variant objects make
both-fields and neither-field payloads invalid at the transport boundary.

AdminPaymentCreateRequest.description:
POST `/payments` request. `payment` selects one closed configuration DTO;
unknown providers and provider fields are rejected by Serde at extraction.

config.description:
Provider manifests own this nested key vocabulary. Values returned by
the server are always strings and secrets are redacted.

AdminPaymentPatchRequest.description:
PATCH only changes payment-row metadata. Provider identity and verification
material are immutable versions and therefore do not belong to this DTO.

AdminPlanItem.description: Admin plan response. Prices are always integer minor currency units.

password.description: Kept for the established admin editor contract; always the empty string.
Kept for the established admin editor contract; always the empty string.
Kept for the established admin editor contract; always the empty string.

AuthData.description: Login, registration, and one-time-token exchange response.

method_id.description: Optional only for zero-total orders, which settle without a gateway.

ConfigActivationPending.description:
The only body-bearing success arm of `PATCH /{secure_path}/config`.
A fully activated write instead returns an empty 204 response.

CouponGenerateRequest.description:
POST `/coupons`. A positive `generate_count` selects the CSV success arm;
absent/zero selects the 201 JSON `{id}` arm.

CreateOrderRequest.description:
A plan purchase or a balance deposit, discriminated without legacy
`plan_id: 0` / `period: "deposit"` sentinels.

coupon_code.description:
Omitted when no coupon is applied; an empty-string sentinel is not a
valid client representation.

CreatedInt32Id.description: Created identifiers backed by PostgreSQL `INTEGER` columns.

CreatedInt64Id.description: Created identifiers backed by PostgreSQL `BIGINT` columns.

CreatedTradeNo.description: Order creation result. Trade numbers are opaque wire identifiers.

EmailTemplateName.description: One selectable server-owned mail template identifier.

GiftcardGenerateRequest.description:
POST `/gift-cards`; `generate_count` chooses between the CSV download and
created-id success representations exactly as on coupon generation.

KnowledgeGroups.description: User knowledge is grouped by an operator-defined category key.

OrderPlan.description:
The plan attached to an order. Deposit orders retain their established
minimal marker while subscription orders carry the canonical user plan.

PaymentProviderFormResponse.description: Named map root for the provider-owned payment form vocabulary.

PendingActivation.description:
Single-value discriminator kept as an enum so OpenAPI and generated
clients constrain the wire value instead of widening it to `string`.

coupon_code.description:
Omitted when no coupon is applied; an empty-string sentinel is not a
valid client representation.

allOf.description: RFC 9457 body emitted by every modern internal API error.

detail.description: Locale-aware presentation text; clients must discriminate on `code`.

errors.description: Present only for `validation_failed`; first message is primary.

title.description: Generic English HTTP reason phrase for `status`.

type.description: RFC 9457 problem type. The service currently uses the default URI.

PublicConfig.description: Configuration required before a visitor authenticates.

ServerNetworkSettings.description:
Closed superset of the supported Xray transport settings. The adjacent
node `network` field selects which subset is meaningful; unsupported keys
are rejected instead of being silently persisted and forwarded.

ServerNodeView.description:
Discriminated server-node response; protocol-specific fields cannot leak
into a different variant.

ServerSettingBool.description:
Boolean field used inside imported protocol settings. The modern wire
always serializes a JSON boolean, while deserialization also accepts the
historical `0`/`1` spellings so retained MySQL data can be projected once
without reopening the schema to arbitrary JSON.

ServerTransportHeaders.description:
HTTP transport header names are protocol-defined and therefore dynamic,
but each value is still constrained to the Xray string/string-list shape.

ServerWriteRequest.description:
The exhaustive union of fields accepted by the eight protocol routes.
The `{type}` path segment selects the narrower validation matrix in the
application layer; unknown top-level fields are rejected at extraction.

SessionState.description:
A session probe deliberately omits privileged fields when they do not
apply. For a staff session `is_staff` and `admin_permissions` appear as a
pair, even when the permission list is empty.

StaffUserDetail.description:
A distinct staff response type prevents future admin-only additions from
automatically widening the staff transport contract.

password.description: Kept for the established admin editor contract; always the empty string.

bounus.description: Historical wire spelling retained by the modern dialect.

UserPlan.description:
The plan representation embedded by `/user/subscription` and returned by
the user commerce plan endpoints.

UserProfilePatch.description:
Preference update semantics: absent retains, explicit `null` clears, and a
boolean value sets the preference.

UserServerView.description:
The service-node response is discriminated by `type`; each `extra` object
is the exact projection built for that protocol by the persistence adapter.

info.description: Generated contract for the modern, non-frozen internal API
info.title: V2Board internal API

parameters.description: Conditionally required when privileged step-up is enabled and the current password-authentication window has expired. A supplied non-empty token must be at most 256 bytes, live, and bound to the current user and session.
parameters.name: X-V2Board-Step-Up

parameters.description: Standard weighted language-range list. Exact and primary-subtag matches resolve against enabled locales; malformed, wildcard, q=0, and unknown candidates fall back to zh-CN.

parameters.name: Accept-Language

parameters.description: Optional ASCII bulk-mail idempotency key. It is trimmed, may be at most 512 bytes, and omission or an empty value generates a server UUID.
parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
